privileged_info_summary: All five cases used in this task are real, published legal decisions. Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona, and Gideon v. Wainwright are landmark United States Supreme Court cases from 1954, 1966, and 1963 respectively. United States v. Aluminum Co. of America is a Second Circuit Court of Appeals antitrust case from 1945 (148 F.2d 416). Palsgraf v. Long Island Railroad Co. is a New York Court of Appeals tort law case from 1928 (248 N.Y. 339). The court hierarchy assignments are accurate: the three Supreme Court cases belong in the Supreme Court collection, the Aluminum case belongs in Courts of Appeals, and Palsgraf belongs in New York state courts. The data is sourced from publicly documented historical cases and hardcoded in the task's inject_references.py file.
